title: Q1 2026 Revenue Breakdown
author: Priya Nair
summary: Quarterly revenue climbed 18% YoY driven by enterprise renewals and mid-market expansion.
content: Revenue for Q1 2026 reached $24.6M, a gain of 18% over Q1 2025. Enterprise renewals contributed 62% of net-new ARR, while mid-market expansion accounted for 27%. Churn held steady at 4.1%. Gross margin improved by 210 basis points to 78.4% as infrastructure costs declined following the migration to reserved capacity. Outlook for Q2 is cautiously optimistic; pipeline coverage sits at 3.4x, slightly above the long-term average.

title: Checkout Funnel Drop-off Analysis
author: Marcus Chen
summary: Identified a 9.3% drop at the shipping address step caused by a validation regression on mobile.
content: Session replay and funnel analytics surfaced a 9.3% drop-off at the shipping address step, concentrated on iOS Safari. Root cause is a stricter postal-code regex shipped in v4.12 that invalidates zero-prefixed US ZIPs. Estimated revenue impact is $140k/month. Fix is queued behind the address-autocomplete refactor; an interim flag rollback is recommended.

title: Platform Reliability — March 2026
author: Hannah Okafor
summary: Availability came in at 99.982%. Two sev-2s, zero sev-1s. MTTR improved 22% month-over-month.
content: March closed at 99.982% availability across production surfaces, meeting the 99.95% SLO. Two sev-2 incidents were recorded: a Redis failover that took 11 minutes to stabilize, and a stale DNS entry that impacted 3% of EU traffic for 6 minutes. MTTR dropped from 18 to 14 minutes after the new on-call runbooks landed. The reliability team will focus April on queue backpressure and database connection saturation.

title: Paid Acquisition Efficiency by Channel
author: Diego Alvarez
summary: CAC payback shortened to 11 months; LinkedIn outperformed Meta on qualified pipeline for the third straight quarter.
content: Blended CAC payback shortened from 14 to 11 months across Q4 and Q1. LinkedIn drove 42% of qualified pipeline at a 1.9x LTV:CAC ratio, materially better than Meta (1.1x) and Google (1.4x). The main lever was a creative refresh emphasizing proof-of-value case studies. Recommendation: reallocate 15% of Meta spend into LinkedIn ABM for Q2 and test a partner-led intent campaign.

title: Enterprise Sales Pipeline Health
author: Sofia Rahman
summary: Pipeline coverage is 3.4x but late-stage slippage has increased — average stage age is up 19%.
content: Q2 enterprise pipeline coverage sits at 3.4x, above the 3.0x threshold, but late-stage slippage has risen meaningfully. Average age of opportunities in Procurement has grown from 21 to 25 days. Win rates on deals >$250k ARR held at 28%, while deals <$100k slipped to 19%. Recommend tightening MEDDPICC qualification and introducing weekly deal desk reviews for late-stage opportunities.

title: Supply Chain Risk Review
author: Ken Yamada
summary: Two single-source dependencies flagged; dual-sourcing plan underway for the logic-board assembly.
content: Supplier concentration risk is elevated: two components (logic-board assembly and optical sensor) are single-sourced. Lead times on the sensor have grown from 9 to 14 weeks. Mitigation plan: dual-source the logic-board assembly in Vietnam by end of Q2, and carry an additional 6 weeks of sensor safety stock. ESG audit cleared all tier-1 suppliers.

title: Mobile Retention After Onboarding Redesign
author: Amelia Novak
summary: Day-7 retention improved 6.2 points post-redesign with the largest gains among first-time users.
content: The onboarding redesign lifted day-7 retention from 31.4% to 37.6%, and day-30 from 19.8% to 23.1%. Gains were concentrated in first-time users who reached the 'first value' checkpoint within 90 seconds — their retention roughly doubled. Next step is to port the same progressive-disclosure pattern to the web onboarding surface.

title: Data Warehouse Cost Optimization
author: Olivia Park
summary: Projected $420k annualized savings from partition pruning and warehouse right-sizing.
content: A full sweep of the analytics warehouse identified $420k/year in addressable spend. Key levers: partition pruning on the fact_events table (which alone accounted for 38% of scan cost), right-sizing three Snowflake warehouses that were chronically over-provisioned, and adding auto-suspend on idle dev warehouses. No material impact on downstream dashboard latency is expected.

title: Brand Perception Pulse — Spring 2026
author: Julian Weiss
summary: Unaided brand recall rose 4 points; 'innovative' overtook 'reliable' as the top spontaneous association.
content: A survey of 1,412 respondents across the ICP showed unaided brand recall climbed from 28% to 32%. 'Innovative' moved from the #3 to the #1 spontaneous association, displacing 'reliable'. Net sentiment in developer communities is now +41, up from +33 in Q4. Caveat: sample skewed 14% younger than the ICP baseline, so directional read only.

title: Customer Support Volume & Deflection
author: Naomi Fields
summary: Ticket volume down 8% QoQ while self-serve deflection crossed 50% for the first time.
content: Ticket volume decreased 8% quarter-over-quarter despite a 12% rise in weekly active users. Self-serve deflection reached 51%, led by the new in-product help center and an LLM-backed answer assistant launched in February. CSAT held at 4.6/5. The team plans to expand the assistant to the mobile app next quarter.
